subject: the smell of a grandmother's kitchen
lyrics:
[Verse]
Flour ghost on a windowsill
Yeast breathing slow in a warming bowl
Cinnamon dust where the light hangs still
Butter melting into something old

[Verse]
A kettle hums one patient note
Apron strings and a clove-stuck ham
The oven door, a held-in breath
Wax cloth folds where the morning swam

[Chorus]
I can find you in the steam
In the cardamom and rain
A doorway made of warm bread air
That opens me again

[Verse]
Dried sage hung from a copper nail
Lemon rind on a tired thumb
The radio low, the screen door pale
And a quiet that the years made numb

[Bridge]
No photograph could hold this room
Only the nose remembers true
A single thread of cooling fruit
And suddenly I'm seven, next to you

[Chorus]
I can find you in the steam
In the cardamom and rain
A doorway made of warm bread air
That opens me again

subject: coming home from the war
lyrics:
[Verse]
The station clock still keeps the time it kept
My boots remember roads they never chose
The bandoneon learns the breath I lost
In rooms where no one counted what it cost

[Verse]
Your shawl, the color of an ending day
The cobblestones rehearse my heavy walk
I carry men who didn't make the train
I carry silence dressed as careful talk

[Chorus]
Dance me slow, the war is in my hands
Hold the part of me that won't come home
My heart still marches where you cannot follow
Lead me back, I've forgotten how to roam

[Verse]
The lamp post leans the way it always leaned
A dog, a wine, a window left ajar
I am the soldier and the empty sleeve
The medal and the wound that won't unscar

[Bridge]
Forgive the way I flinch at slamming doors
Forgive the nights I'm someone else's name
Teach my feet the country of your floor
Teach me peace the way you taught me flame

[Chorus]
Dance me slow, the war is in my hands
Hold the part of me that won't come home
My heart still marches where you cannot follow
Lead me back, I've forgotten how to roam

subject: a long-haul trucker calling home at 3am
lyrics:
[Verse]
Three in the morning, payphone glow
I-80 humming, ninety miles to go
Got a quarter and a dial tone and your sleepy hello
Diesel in my collar, snow on the radio

[Chorus]
Baby I'm a thousand miles of yellow line
Talking to a kitchen that ain't mine
Tell the kids their daddy held the phone too long
And hung up before the morning made me strong

[Verse]
Coffee gone to acid, dash lights low
A deer-eyed truck stop where the lonely go
You said you left the porch light on for me
I said I'd be there 'fore the coffee's free

[Verse]
My hands are made of rubber and of road
My back's a map of every hill I towed
And your voice come crackling down the wire so thin
It's the only home I ever sleep within

[Bridge]
Nine more states and a weigh station moon
I'll be in your doorway sometime soon
Don't wait up, but leave the hallway lit
Three a.m.'s the church where I confess and sit

[Chorus]
Baby I'm a thousand miles of yellow line
Talking to a kitchen that ain't mine
Tell the kids their daddy held the phone too long
And hung up before the morning made me strong

subject: a cursed cargo on a long voyage
lyrics:
[Verse]
Well the crate came aboard on a moonless night (shoo-bop)
The captain said don't ask, just hold it tight
The compass spun a waltz it never learned
And every candle in the galley burned

[Chorus]
Oh, cargo, cargo, what'd we load?
(doo-wop, doo-wop, down the road)
The sea won't sleep and neither will we
Whatever's in the hold is calling me

[Verse]
The seagulls left us forty leagues from shore
The water turned a color seen before
The cook he sang and then forgot his name
And the lantern light came back a different flame

[Verse]
We knock on wood, we kiss the iron rail
We count the rats that vanished off the tail
The first mate swears the crate began to hum
A lullaby for sailors yet to come

[Bridge]
Throw it over! cried the bos'n true
But the rope it tied itself to me and you
Now the horizon's just a closing door
And the sea keeps singing shoo-bop, evermore

[Chorus]
Oh, cargo, cargo, what'd we load?
(doo-wop, doo-wop, down the road)
The sea won't sleep and neither will we
Whatever's in the hold is calling me

subject: a tailor who dresses the whole block
lyrics:
[Verse]
Needle in the lamplight, thread between my teeth
I know the secret measure of the man beneath
The banker's crooked shoulder, the widow's mended hem
I cut the cloth of Sunday for the lot of them

[Chorus]
I dress the whole block, button up its pride
Chalk line on the trouble that a suit can hide
They come in bent and broken, leave 'em standing tall
I'm the man with pins, who fits us all

[Verse]
The preacher's funeral collar, the gambler's lucky vest
The boy's first job-day jacket, pressed and double-blessed
I've let out seams for sorrow, took 'em in for joy
Stitched a wedding into every grown-up boy

[Verse]
Mirror in the corner's seen a hundred years
Of grown men turning slow and wiping happy tears
My own coat's got a patch where the elbow wore away
The tailor never tailors his own gray

[Bridge]
When they lay me down they'll wear the things I sewed
A whole block of my fingerprints walking down the road
No monument, no marble, just a perfect seam
And the way a tired man looks better than his dream

[Chorus]
I dress the whole block, button up its pride
Chalk line on the trouble that a suit can hide
They come in bent and broken, leave 'em standing tall
I'm the man with pins, who fits us all

subject: a chess hustler in the park
lyrics:
[Verse]
Five dollars on the clock, old man
The pigeons keep the time we can't
A folding table, a kingdom drawn in squares
The whole world walking past, nobody cares

[Verse]
He smiles like a trap with a velvet hinge
Lets you take the bishop, lets you think you win
The city roars behind the iron gate
He pushes one small pawn and seals your fate

[Chorus]
Check, the afternoon is falling slow
Check, he's seen this end a thousand goes
The sun comes down like a toppled king
And the silence after means everything

[Verse]
Tourists, students, drunks, and millionaires
They all sit down and leave a little scared
He doesn't keep their money, keeps their faces
A gallery of all their losing places

[Bridge]
And when the lamps come on the board goes still
He stays a little longer than he will
Replaying every move the day forgot
The only opponent he never beat — the clock

[Chorus]
Check, the afternoon is falling slow
Check, he's seen this end a thousand goes
The sun comes down like a toppled king
And the silence after means everything

subject: taping up the last box when you move out
lyrics:
[Verse]
The tape pulls long and the room goes thin
Echo where the sofa's dent had been
A square of cleaner paint on the wall
The size of nothing, the size of all

[Chorus]
One more box, one strip of brown
Fold the flaps of this whole town
The light comes empty through the blind
Goodbye to the corner I left behind

[Verse]
A bottle cap, a hairpin, a key
To a door that won't belong to me
The radiator clicks its last advice
The quiet here was almost nice

[Verse]
My footsteps sound like someone new
Already learning to walk on through
I write the word KITCHEN on the side
Though kitchens are the things you can't provide

[Bridge]
Soft, so soft, the marker squeaks
Naming all the ordinary weeks
Tomorrow it's a stranger's afternoon
Tonight it's mine, beneath a borrowed moon

[Chorus]
One more box, one strip of brown
Fold the flaps of this whole town
The light comes empty through the blind
Goodbye to the corner I left behind

subject: losing your faith and finding it again
lyrics:
[Verse]
I used to kneel where the candles glow (oo-wee-oo)
Then the prayers got quiet and the lights got low
I walked out the door of the house of stone
And I learned how to whisper to the dark alone

[Chorus]
But you came back, you came back (shoo-bop bop)
Like a streetlight humming on the midnight track
I lost my faith on a Tuesday gray
And found it in a stranger's smile today

[Verse]
No angel came with a trumpet sound
Just a kid who helped an old man off the ground
A bus that waited, a hand held out
And the smallest reason to forget my doubt

[Verse]
I didn't find the steeple, found the street
The holy in the ordinary beat
The choir was a kettle and a kid's first word
The sermon was the realest thing I heard

[Bridge]
Maybe heaven's not a place to climb (oo-wee-oo)
Maybe it's the people for a little time
I stopped my searching and I learned to stay
And the faith I lost came home to stay

[Chorus]
But you came back, you came back (shoo-bop bop)
Like a streetlight humming on the midnight track
I lost my faith on a Tuesday gray
And found it in a stranger's smile today

subject: the last day of summer before college
lyrics:
[Verse]
The pool drains slow and the cicadas burn
One suitcase open like a question we won't turn
Your bicycle leans on its rusted final day
The street still warm where our shadows used to play

[Chorus]
Dance me through the last of August heat
Before the train divides us at our feet
Hold the night, the night won't hold for us
Tomorrow's a goodbye we won't discuss

[Verse]
The sprinkler ticks its old familiar round
The porch light keeps a secret with no sound
Your mother's calling but you don't go in
You let the summer end against my skin

[Verse]
We carve our names where no one ever looks
We trade our futures like forbidden books
The ice cream truck has driven down its last
And everything is suddenly the past

[Bridge]
Don't say the word, the word that splits the year
Just press your forehead, breathe and stay right here
The streetlamp flickers, counting down our song
We were children for exactly this long

[Chorus]
Dance me through the last of August heat
Before the train divides us at our feet
Hold the night, the night won't hold for us
Tomorrow's a goodbye we won't discuss

subject: a cursed cargo on a long voyage
lyrics:
[Verse]
We loaded up the hold in the harbor town
A crate with no markings, weigh the whole ship down
The old man warned us, leave it on the shore
But the captain wanted gold, so we sailed for more

[Chorus]
Oh-oh, the cargo cry at night
Oh-oh, no moon, no light
We a-sail on a sea that don't forgive
Carry the curse just to make a living

[Verse]
The wind it stop, the compass it spin
A cold come creeping from the box within
The cook he sweating in the freezing air
And nobody go down the ladder there

[Verse]
Jah know the water, Jah know the deep
But the thing in the hold it never sleep
We drum on the deck and we sing for dawn
Keep the rhythm going till the dark is gone

[Bridge]
Throw it in the ocean! the first mate plead
But the chain hold tight to a sailor's greed
Now we drift forever on the cursed blue
Learn the lesson late: what you carry, carry you

[Chorus]
Oh-oh, the cargo cry at night
Oh-oh, no moon, no light
We a-sail on a sea that don't forgive
Carry the curse just to make a living

subject: texting someone you shouldn't
lyrics:
[Verse]
It's a quarter past the line I swore I'd hold
Thumb a-hovering over fire in the cold
Three little dots like a copperhead's eye
Waiting in the grass for a passerby

[Chorus]
Oh I shouldn't, but I'm gonna, Lord I know
The ridge is steep and the river's down below
One more message and the dam'll surely break
I'm a fool with a phone and a heart on the take

[Verse]
Delete it, type it, set the cursor free
The banjo of my pulse won't let me be
She's a back-road shortcut to a world of harm
A wildfire dressed up like a homestead charm

[Verse]
My mama raised me better than my hands
That keep on building castles out of sand
The porch is dark, the whole house is asleep
And I'm sowing in a field I ought not reap

[Bridge]
Send, and the night swallows the deed
A little seed of trouble takes its lead
Tomorrow I'll be reaping what I sowed
A lonesome harvest on a dead-end road

[Chorus]
Oh I shouldn't, but I'm gonna, Lord I know
The ridge is steep and the river's down below
One more message and the dam'll surely break
I'm a fool with a phone and a heart on the take

subject: running a pirate radio station
lyrics:
[Verse]
Attic antenna wrapped in copper prayer
A mattress on the window for the cops out there
We broadcast from a frequency they swear don't exist
Four watts of rebellion and a borrowed playlist

[Chorus]
This is the signal, this is the ghost
The song they tried to bury, hosting coast to coast
Turn your dial to the empty place
We're the static with a human face

[Verse]
We play the records that the suits won't spin
Dedications to the kids who can't fit in
A caller in the dark says you saved my night
And the meter on the board glows alien bright

[Verse]
The van outside is circling once again
We pull the plug and scatter like the wind
But the tower's just a closet and a stubborn dream
You can't arrest a thing as small as a beam

[Bridge]
Somewhere a teenager presses record
Catching every word the city ignored
We're nothing, we're nowhere, we're three in the morning
And we're louder than the silence they were forming

[Chorus]
This is the signal, this is the ghost
The song they tried to bury, hosting coast to coast
Turn your dial to the empty place
We're the static with a human face

subject: being talked over in every meeting
lyrics:
[Verse]
I raised my little hand at a quarter past nine (bom-bom)
Had a golden idea and it lit up my mind
But the room kept rolling like a runaway train
And my voice fell out like a drop of rain

[Chorus]
Talk me over, talk me down (shoo-bop)
I'm the quietest ghost in this whole town
I said it first but they heard it from Dale
Now he's getting the raise and I'm telling the tale

[Verse]
I cleared my throat at the conference phone
Got a mute-button heart and a microphone
Somebody's chiming, somebody's loud
And I'm waving a flag in a talking crowd

[Verse]
My good idea wore a borrowed suit
Walked right out in another man's boot
And the minutes will say that I wasn't there
Just an empty seat and a swivel chair

[Bridge]
But I'll come back Monday with a steady drum (bom-bom)
Gonna finish my sentence 'fore the next one come
Gonna plant my flag in the middle of the room
And bloom, little wallflower, bloom bloom bloom

[Chorus]
Talk me over, talk me down (shoo-bop)
I'm the quietest ghost in this whole town
I said it first but they heard it from Dale
Now he's getting the raise and I'm telling the tale

subject: running a pirate radio station
lyrics:
[Verse]
Basement bunker, wires taped to the vent
Four-watt prophet, every dollar already spent
FCC ghosts circling the block in white vans
I'm the frequency they can't fit in their plans
Antenna on the rooftop like a middle-finger steeple
Signal for the unsigned, the unheard people
Turn the dial past the corporate megaphone
Find a crackle and a heartbeat — that's home

[Chorus]
We pirate, we ghost in the wire
Broadcast a riot, the static catch fire
No license, no leash, no logo, no boss
Just a voice in the dark sayin' you ain't lost

[Verse]
Midnight requests from a kid who can't sleep
Dedicate a beat to the promises we keep
They raid the address but the address is a myth
I moved the whole studio in a duffel and a kiss
Mixtape gospel, underground cathedral
Every transmission is illegal but it's needle
Straight in the vein of a city gone numb
I'm the pulse they forgot, here's where it's from

[Bridge]
They can jam the tower but they can't jam the want
Every silenced station leaves a haunt
Press record, little soldier, catch the wave
What they bury underground is what they couldn't save

[Chorus]
We pirate, we ghost in the wire
Broadcast a riot, the static catch fire
No license, no leash, no logo, no boss
Just a voice in the dark sayin' you ain't lost

subject: the last day of summer before college
lyrics:
[Verse]
The creek runs low where we learned how to swim
A tire swing creaks on a busted limb
Your daddy's truck got the tailgate down
Last slice of summer in this one-stop town

[Chorus]
So play me one more on the porch tonight
Before the morning takes us out of sight
The fireflies blink like they're saying so long
One more turn of the old summer song

[Verse]
We packed the trunk with a quilt and a prayer
Mama tucked a twenty she could barely spare
The gravel road knows the way we go
But it don't know how to tell us no

[Verse]
The cornfield's tall and the church bell's old
Gonna trade these hills for a world I'm told
Is bigger and brighter and farther than home
But my roots run deep in this Tennessee loam

[Bridge]
Don't you cry now, fiddle, don't you moan
We're just children grown and heading on
I'll carry the holler wherever I roam
A pocket of dirt to remind me of home

[Chorus]
So play me one more on the porch tonight
Before the morning takes us out of sight
The fireflies blink like they're saying so long
One more turn of the old summer song
